PURPOSE

The thermoduric plate count is used to obtain an assessment of milking machine hygiene.
Thermoduric bacteria are most commonly found in milkstone, milk deposits in the milking plant
and vat and perished rubberware.

SCOPE

Samples selected by the sample selection programme as required by me

title: 4.0 - Plating and Counting Section V7
preview:
The coliform plate count is used to obtain an assessment of the basic hygiene standards in the
milking shed. A high coliform count indicates either poor cleaning technique and/or inadequate
hot water being used to clean the plant.
A true coliform plate count requires confirmation of doubtful colifor

title: 4.0 - Plating and Counting Section V7
preview:
The sample selection programme will allocate each sample to a 50-position matrix. This
allows the traceability of each sample to its controls and the incubation records kept for each
tray. Each sample need not be kept in the exact matrix position specified but must be kept
in the specified matrix.

All Non-Protein Nitrogen analyses are to be performed by either a trained technician or a
technician who is under the supervision of a trained technician.
